analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 complete (counterparty IDs, ROUND_4 days 1-3). Read round4work/suggested direction.txt Phase 1, round4description.txt, outputs/r4_initial_analysis_summary.txt. Ran run_r4_phase1_counterparty.py (forward mids, aggressor inference, bursts, graph, baseline residuals).
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate satisfied; see round4_phase1_complete for per-bullet paths, top hypotheses, and nulls. Proceed to Phase 2 burst-conditioned rules + execution tests when instructed.

round4_phase1_complete.methodology: Forward mid: K-th strictly later price snapshot for same (day,symbol) after trade ts; concurrent book via last snapshot with ts<=trade ts. Aggressor: trade price>=ask => buyer_agg; price<=bid => seller_agg; else ambig. Horizons K in {5,20,100}. Hour bucket = (ts//10000)%24. Spread bin on traded symbol: 0<=2, 1:3-5, 2>=6. Burst = >=3 trades same (day,timestamp).

bullets.conclusion: Pooled K=20 screen: Mark 67 buyer_agg on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mean fwd mid ~+1.81 (n=164, t~5.86); day-split +2.03 / +1.45 / +2.02 on days 1/2/3. Mark 49 buyer_agg extract n=105 mean ~+1.83. **Extension:** day-block bootstrap (2000× resample 3 days with replacement) — Mark 67 buyer_agg extract pooled mean CI95 ~[1.45, 2.03] (see bootstrap JSON); Mark 55 buyer_agg extract CI excludes 0 ~[0.33, 0.57].

bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (buyer_agg, fwd20, cell mean by buyer,seller,symbol)
bullets.conclusion: Residuals vs cell means: mean ~0, stdev ~6 (n=1465 buyer_agg).

Mark 01 -> Mark 22 dominates directed counts. **Reciprocity:** Mark14↔Mark38 and Mark14↔Mark55 are **~0.95+** reciprocity (balanced duopoly-style); **Mark01→Mark22** has **0** reverse edges (one-way basket). **Adjacent two-hop (prior extension):** not a positive extract lead. See r4_twohop_adjacent_extract_fwd20.json.
Pooled burst extract fwd20 modestly positive vs control; day-split mixed.

**Proxy:** passive side = non-aggressor on traded BBO; K=20 fwd on **traded** mid. **Worst** (n≥20): Mark14 passive on **VEV_5300** mean **−0.25** (n=28); Mark01 passive on **VEV_5300** **−0.12** (n=132). **Best** passive: Mark22/Mark49 on **extract** mean **~+1.2 to +1.7**. Population: avoid quoting as passive into names that are systematically lifted against on 5300 (qualitative).

negative_or_null_findings: Mean-centered residuals vs buyer_agg cell means ~0.
Mark 22 buyer_agg extract sparse per day.
Adjacent-trade two-hop middle-match motif: extract fwd20 at second leg **worse** than control (see r4_twohop_adjacent_extract_fwd20.json) — not a tradeable positive chain signal.

Passive-side proxy: Mark 14 (and 01) on **VEV_5300** as passive show **negative** mean K=20 on traded mid — possible adverse selection to aggressive flow on that strike; extract passive Mark22/Mark49 still positive (same file).

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2: run_r4_phase2_analysis.py — burst×Mark01→Mark22 VEV fwd5300, joint-gate×burst extract fwd20, extract microprice×spread×fwd10, signed aggressive-flow vs fwd20 corr; trader_v1 burst+joint-gate passive extract backtest (day inferred from open mid).
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate: see round4_phase2_complete. v1 sim shows +1023 total (worse=all) — execution layer can be positive but needs more days/out-of-sample and inventory control.

interaction_with_phase1.burst_extract: Phase 1 pooled burst vs control showed mild extract mid lift; Phase 2 conditions M01→M22 VEV bursts: VEV_5300 fwd20 mean ~+0.36 (n=317) — refines Phase-1 null on unconditional Mark01→22 5300 (-0.08) by isolating burst timestamps.
interaction_with_phase1.joint_gate: Phase 2 joint tight (5200+5300<=2) at M01→M22 burst times: only n=7 tight vs n=310 wide for extract fwd20; tight mean higher (~+0.71 vs ~+0.27) but tiny n — Sonic gate 'cleans' only a rare slice here (partial support, not statistically tight).
interaction_with_phase1.microprice: Orthogonal to counterparty: tight extract spread bin shows large mean fwd10 mid (+2.0 on n=186) vs negative in wide bins — confirms spread regime matters for mid dynamics (inclineGod-style).
interaction_with_phase1.residuals: Signed aggressive net flow vs extract fwd20 corr ~0.12 (n=764) — weak linear link; does not replace pair/burst structure.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Extract microprice regime: when L1 spread<=2, mean fwd10 extract mid strongly positive vs wide-spread regimes (r4_p2_extract_microprice_spread_fwd10.json).
M01→M22 VEV burst timestamps predict positive VEV_5300 fwd20 mid (K=20) vs Phase-1 unconditional negative.
Sim: trader_v1 passive long extract on (tape burst list + live joint tight) yields +1023 total PnL Round 4 days 1-3 under worse and all matching.
Signed aggressive extract flow vs fwd20 mid: small positive correlation (~0.12).
Joint-gate×burst extract subsample (n=7 tight) — hypothesis only; do not size trades on this alone.

negative_or_null: Joint-tight at burst times is rare (7/317); most burst edge is not 'Sonic-cleaned' in this tape slice.
Day-stability of burst extract lift remains mixed from Phase 1; Phase 2 did not fully re-split every new metric by day.
trader_v1 relies on tape-derived burst list + open-mid day inference — fragile vs regime drift; Phase 3 should re-evaluate with explicit gate replication from R3 script.
round4_phase2_complete.phase3_gate: Phase 2 complete; Phase 3 may run Sonic joint gate replication on R4 with counterparty interaction.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 3: run_r4_phase3_sonic_gate.py — R3-aligned inner-join panel (5200,5300,extract), tight=(s5200<=2)&(s5300<=2), K=20 fwd extract; per-day Welch tight vs loose; inclineGod spread–spread corr tight vs loose; Mark01→Mark22 VEV trades merged to panel; tight∩burst overlap; trader_v2 joint-gate-only backtest vs v1.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate: round4_phase3_complete. Sonic joint gate on R4 tape strongly lifts extract fwd20 vs loose (pooled Welch); inclineGod spread co-movement higher in tight-only on day 1; Mark01→22 VEV prints almost always occur under tight book so counterparty×gate Welch is dominated by n=3 loose tail — use overlap table instead. Sim: gate-only v2 >> burst+tight v1 on this slice.

round4_phase3_complete.r3_convention: Same as analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py: inner join timestamps, dedupe per product per ts, spread=ask1-bid1, tight if both <=2, fwd20 = shift(-20) on extract mid.

comparison_to_phase1_2_without_full_gate.extract_fwd20_tight_vs_loose: Pooled (r4_p3_spread_spread_inclinegod.json): mean_fwd20_tight ~+0.32 vs loose ~-0.12, Welch t~7.94, p~2.3e-15 (n_tight=7706, n_loose=22234). This directly supports Sonic's 'cleaner under tight' on **mid** forward moves — much stronger than Phase-2 burst-only conditioning alone.
comparison_to_phase1_2_without_full_gate.inclineGod_spread_spread: Day 1: corr(s5200,s5300) ~0.51 when tight-only vs ~0.02 loose-only (r4_p3_joint_gate_per_day.json). Pooled tight corr ~0.17 vs loose ~0.08 — co-movement visible but regime-dependent.
comparison_to_phase1_2_without_full_gate.counterparty_x_gate: Mark01→Mark22 on VEV at trade ts: 1336/1339 rows have joint tight; only 3 loose — Welch tight vs loose on extract fwd20 at those prints is meaningless (r4_p3_m01_m22_vev_gate_extract_fwd20.json). **Gate does not 'reveal' Mark01→22** — basket prints mechanically sit in tight books. Use r4_p3_tight_burst_overlap.json: only ~3–7% of tight timestamps are multi-trade burst timestamps per day.
comparison_to_phase1_2_without_full_gate.three_way_burst_gate: r4_p3_three_way_burst_gate_pair.json: essentially all M01→22 VEV prints in our burst_pairs set are tight+burst (n=1336); non-burst M01→22 VEV at same merge is empty — burst list was defined from those prints, so this cell is tautological unless burst definition broadened.

tier_a_ranked_edges.edge: Sonic joint gate on R4: extract K=20 forward mid is materially higher when tight vs not, stable Welch across csv days 1–3 (see r4_p3_joint_gate_per_day.json + pooled r4_p3_spread_spread_inclinegod.json).
tier_a_ranked_edges.evidence: pooled p~2e-15; day1 t~5.32 p~1e-7; day2 t~4.24 p~2e-5; day3 t~6.78 p~1e-11

tier_a_ranked_edges.edge: Sim PnL: trader_v2 (joint gate only, passive extract) total +13,236 vs trader_v1 (burst list + gate) +1,023 on Round 4 days 1–3 (worse=all).
tier_a_ranked_edges.evidence: results.json iteration 2; burst filter reduced fills vs gate-only in this implementation

tier_a_ranked_edges.edge: Tight timestamps vs burst timestamps overlap is small (r4_p3_tight_burst_overlap.json): e.g. day1 only 98/1381 tight ts are burst ts — Sonic gate is mostly **not** the same object as 'burst'.

Phase-1 VEV_5300 unconditional Mark01→22 negative mid fwd20 is **not** contradicted by extract fwd20 at the same trade timestamp under tight (different asset); compare r4_focal_pair_vev5300.json vs r4_p3_m01_m22_vev_gate_extract_fwd20.json.
tier_a_ranked_edges.evidence: mean_fwd20_extract when tight at those prints ~+0.30 (n=1336)

tier_a_ranked_edges.edge: Phase-2 microprice tight-bin extract fwd10 (+2.0 on n=186) aligns with Phase-3 gate story (spread regime); keep as complementary not duplicate.

round4_phase3_complete.does_gate_clean_signal_like_sonic: Yes for **population** extract fwd20 (strong Welch tight vs loose). No for **Mark01→22 identification** under naive merge — those prints are almost always already tight, so the gate does not separate counterparty states; burst vs non-burst is the more meaningful orthogonal axis for that pair.
round4_phase3_complete.phase4_suggestion: Next: strategies that trade **on** joint-tight episodes (v2-style) with inventory/risk caps; re-test Mark conditioning with burst definitions independent of M01→22 VEV (e.g. all >=3-trade bursts) and day-held-out.

analysis_description: Post-Phase3 evidence: leave-one-csv-day-out Welch on joint-gate extract fwd20 (same K=20 as Phase 3); extract counterparty notional under tight vs loose at trade ts; trader_v3 inventory skew ablation vs v2.
used_to_decide: LOO: pooled tight vs loose Welch remains strongly positive when any single csv day is excluded (t_stat 6.66–7.10, p~1e-11–1e-12); per-day tight mean fwd20 > loose for all three days. Counterparty: under joint-tight timestamps, tape extract trades concentrate notional in Mark14↔Mark55 and Mark55↔Mark01 pairs (r4_counterparty_extract_under_tight.json). Sim: naive passive skew at |pos|>40 (v3) cuts PnL vs v2 (+2961 worse vs +13236) — inventory skew fights the long-only edge; prefer flat lean or asymmetric caps tuned to sim.

round4_post_phase3_notes.loo_joint_gate_fwd20: r4_loo_joint_gate_welch_fwd20.json: exclude day 1/2/3 from pooled sample — tight mean fwd20 stays positive vs negative loose; all three LOO t-stats > 0; all three per-day-only Welch also tight>loose means.
round4_post_phase3_notes.counterparty_under_tight: Inner-join trade ts to price-panel tight flag: 351 extract trades at tight ts vs 1030 at loose (days 1–3). Top notional tight pairs: Mark14→Mark55, Mark55→Mark14, Mark55→Mark01, Mark01→Mark55.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v3_vs_v2: v3 adds passive sells when long>40 and buys when short<-40 under the same gate; worse total_profit 2961 vs v2 13236 — do not ship this skew without redesign.

analysis_description: Counterparty × Sonic gate (Phase-1 horizons): extract fwd markouts by buyer→seller pair, split by joint-tight at trade ts (run_r4_pair_markout_joint_gate.py). Sim: trader_v4 position-aware buy clip only vs v2 fixed Q=4.
used_to_decide: Population tight-vs-loose extract fwd20 remains the main edge (Phase 3). Conditioning on (pair, tight) for **tape** extract trades: top tight-K20 pairs are Mark55↔Mark14 and Mark55↔Mark01 (n~70–86 each). Mark14→Mark55 K=20: mean tight +0.18 (n=84) vs loose -0.39 (n=232) — directionally aligned with gate story but Welch t_approx ~1.05 (underpowered). Mark55→Mark14: both regimes positive mean K=20 (~+0.44 tight vs +0.62 loose) — gate does not separate that pair on this horizon. Execution: v4 (clip buys by |pos|) reaches +7329 worse vs v2 +13236 — clipping reduces capture vs fixed Q; keep v2-style sizing or tune thresholds with tape-based risk only.

round4_post_phase3_notes.pair_extract_fwd_joint_gate: r4_pair_extract_fwd_joint_gate.json — Phase-1 snap_at + forward_mid K∈{5,20,100}; joint_tight from concurrent 5200/5300 spreads. Pooled tables for top pairs by tight-K20 count; per-day breakdown included.
round4_post_phase3_notes.focal_Mark14_to_55_k20: tight mean ~+0.18 (n=84) vs loose ~-0.39 (n=232); t_approx ~1.05 — not statistically strong at pair level.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v4_vs_v2: v4 worse total 7329 vs v2 13236; more trades (~4433 vs 4365) but smaller edge per inventory path — fixed Q=4 wins on this backtest slice.

analysis_description: Tape: (1) extract trades stratified by aggressor (price vs concurrent extract BBO) × joint-tight × K∈{5,20,100}; (2) all-symbol burst (>=3 trades same ts) × joint gate vs balanced non-burst control, extract fwd20. Sim: trader_v5 flat Q boost vs v2.
used_to_decide: Aggressor×gate: buyer_agg K=20 mean is **lower** when joint-tight (~+0.47, n=190) than loose (~+0.82, n=579); seller_agg K=20 is **higher** when tight (~+0.065, n=161) vs loose (~-0.24, n=451) — asymmetric; passive buy logic is not the same cell as tape buyer_agg. All-symbol bursts: **317/318** burst timestamps are joint-tight (only 1 loose on day 3); burst extract fwd20 pooled mean ~+0.29 vs control sample ~+0.095 — aligns Phase-2 burst story but gate rarely splits bursts here. v5 (Q=6 if |pos|<=15 else 4): worse **13,245** vs v2 **13,236** — negligible; keep v2 simplicity.

round4_post_phase3_notes.aggressor_gate_markout: r4_extract_aggressor_gate_markout.json — per-day tables included; ambig n=0 at extract (trades at touch).
round4_post_phase3_notes.burst_all_syms_gate: r4_burst_all_syms_gate_extract_fwd20.json — burst timestamps co-occur with Sonic tight ~99.7% in this tape slice.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v5_vs_v2: Flat Q boost ~flat PnL vs v2; not worth added complexity on this run.

analysis_description: Tape: (1) joint-gate *persistence* — at tight timestamps, extract fwd20 when gate still tight 20 snapshots ahead vs flipped loose (aligned panel); (2) buyer_agg extract residuals vs (buyer,seller) cell mean, split by joint-tight. Sim: trader_v6 adds extract L1<=2 filter on top of v2.
used_to_decide: **Persistence (r4_joint_gate_persist_extract_fwd20.json):** Among rows that are joint-tight *now* with valid fwd20 and future tight flag, mean extract fwd20 is **lower** when the gate **remains** tight 20 steps ahead (~+0.01, n=3532) than when it **opens** to loose by then (~+0.59, n=4174); pooled Welch t≈−5.93, p~3e−9. So the *population* tight>loose edge is not 'staying inside tight longer predicts higher fwd' on this construction — forward move is larger when the surface **exits** joint-tight over the horizon (interpret with care: conditioning on future state). **Buyer_agg residuals × gate:** pooled mean residual under joint-tight **−0.13** (n=190) vs **+0.04** loose (n=578) — aggressive buys under tight voucher books **underperform** their own (buyer,seller) baseline vs loose. **Sim v6** (triple: joint gate + extract spread≤2): worse **220** vs v2 **13,236** — extra extract filter **removes** most of the sim edge; do not stack that filter for this passive style.

round4_post_phase3_notes.gate_persist_fwd20: r4_joint_gate_persist_extract_fwd20.json — per-day Welch stay vs leave included.
round4_post_phase3_notes.buyer_agg_residual_joint_gate: r4_residuals_buyer_agg_joint_gate.json — baseline from full-tape cells n>=5 same symbol as Phase 1.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v6_vs_v2: Triple microstructure collapses PnL; v2 remains the sim baseline.

analysis_description: Tape: seller_agg extract residuals vs (buyer,seller) cell mean × joint gate (mirror of iteration-6 buyer_agg); hour-of-day × joint gate Welch on aligned extract fwd20 (all 24 hours n>=30 tight+loose). Sim: trader_v7 long cap 72 vs v2.
used_to_decide: **Seller_agg residuals (r4_residuals_seller_agg_joint_gate.json):** Under **joint-tight**, mean residual **+0.24** (n=161) vs **−0.09** loose (n=449) — opposite sign pattern to **buyer_agg** (tight was worse than baseline for buyers). Suggests seller-side tape prints are **less bad vs their pair mean** when vouchers are tight. **Hour × gate (r4_hour_joint_gate_extract_fwd20.json):** All 24 hours have tight & loose n≥30; **12 hours** show tight mean fwd20 > loose with **p<0.05** (e.g. hours 5,8,14,21 strong t-stats); **6 hours** have tight mean **below** loose — regime useful but **not uniform** intraday. **trader_v7** (no buys when pos≥72): worse **5,924** vs v2 **13,236** — capping long **removes** profitable pyramiding; keep v2.

round4_post_phase3_notes.seller_agg_residual_joint_gate: Baseline built from seller_agg-only cells n>=5; same K=20 and snap_at as Phase 1.
round4_post_phase3_notes.hour_gate_stability: Welch tight vs loose per hour on pooled days 1–3 panel — see JSON for full table.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v7_vs_v2: Long cap 72 underperforms unconstrained v2 on this backtest.

analysis_description: Day×hour joint-gate Welch stability (per csv_day); Mark01→Mark22 on any VEV_* → extract fwd markouts × joint-tight; trader_v8 = joint gate only in pooled-significant hours.
used_to_decide: **Day×hour (r4_day_hour_gate_welch_fwd20.json):** With min n (tight≥10, loose≥25) per cell, **no hour** has **all three** csv days with Welch **tight>loose** and **p<0.1** — pooled-hour significance is **not** uniformly day-stable at this threshold. Hours where **all 3 days** have tight mean fwd20 **>** loose mean (ignoring p): **3, 5, 9, 11, 13, 21** (subset of pooled winners). **Mark01→Mark22 VEV prints:** n=**1339**; **1336/1339** at joint-tight; K=20 extract fwd mean **~+0.30** when tight vs **+2.0** on n=**3** loose-only prints (Phase 3 overlap story). **trader_v8** (trade only in hours {3,5,6,7,8,11,12,13,14,16,21,22} from pooled p<0.05 table + joint tight): worse **5,820** vs v2 **13,236** — hour filter **removes** too much profitable tight time; do not ship pooled-hour mask without per-day calibration.

round4_post_phase3_notes.day_hour_stability: rollup_by_hour: days_tight_gt_loose_and_p_lt_0.1 counts 0..3 per hour — none reach 3.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v8_vs_v2: Pooled-significant hour whitelist underperforms v2; full-clock joint gate wins in sim.

analysis_description: Orchestration: Mark01→Mark22 VEV timestamps vs same-timestamp extract prints (lead–lag at dt=0); sim trader_v9 = joint gate + blacklist hours {1,2,10} where pooled Welch had tight<loose p<0.05.
used_to_decide: **Same-ts orchestration (r4_orchestration_m01_m22_vev_extract_same_ts.json):** **317** unique (day,timestamp) with M01→M22 on some **VEV_***; only **14** (**4.4%**) also have an **extract** trade at the **same** ts. Extract **fwd20** from that clock: mean **~+0.21** when extract co-prints (n=14) vs **~+0.28** when VEV-only at that ts (n=303) — **no** evidence here that co-printing extract predicts **higher** fwd; small-n on overlap. **trader_v9** (skip hours 1,2,10 under joint gate): worse **10,598** vs v2 **13,236** — blacklist **hurts** vs full v2 but **less** than v8 whitelist (**5,820**); still not an improvement over v2.

round4_post_phase3_notes.m01_m22_vev_extract_same_ts: Phase-1 snap_at + forward_mid K=20 on extract series at the basket timestamp.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v9_vs_v2_v8: Bad-hour filter recovers some PnL vs good-hour-only v8 but remains below unconstrained v2.

analysis_description: Tape: all VEV_* trades — forward mid on traded strike at K∈{5,20,100}, split by joint-tight at trade ts; per-strike Welch K=20 when n>=25. Sim: trader_v10 = joint gate + extract L1>=3 (wide extract book).
used_to_decide: **VEV trade markouts × gate (r4_vev_trade_fwd_joint_gate.json):** Pooled all strikes **K=20**: mean fwd on traded mid **~+0.056** when joint-tight (n=1549) vs **~−0.125** when loose (n=327) — directionally favors tight but **Welch p~0.45** (not significant; imbalanced loose n). Only **VEV_4000** has both arms **n≥25** for K=20 Welch; there tight mean **~+0.47** vs loose **~−0.13**, **p~0.19**. Other strikes lack loose-side count for stable per-strike tests. **trader_v10** (joint tight + **extract spread ≥3**): worse **13,044** vs v2 **13,236** (**−192**), **4,364** trades — **closest** microstructure variant to v2 so far (contrast v6 tight extract **+220**). Wide extract under Sonic gate is **not** toxic like ultra-tight filter.

round4_post_phase3_notes.vev_fwd_joint_gate: Forward on the traded voucher's own mid series (not extract).
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v10_vs_v2: Small PnL gap vs v2; optional refinement only.

analysis_description: Lead–lag: after Mark01→Mark22 VEV print, extract fwd20 vs next extract trade within W (timestamp units); top extract pairs in long window. Sim: trader_v11 joint gate + extract spread exactly 2–3.
used_to_decide: **Lead–lag (r4_m01_m22_vev_extract_leadlag_fwd20.json):** n=1339 M01→M22 VEV events with valid extract fwd20. **Very short W=200:** next extract in (ts, ts+200] has **lower** mean fwd20 (**−0.26**, n=118) than no-soon-extract (**+0.35**, n=1221) — possible microstructure **pressure** in the immediate tick neighborhood (interpret cautiously). **W=1000:** soon-extract mean **+0.38** vs **+0.25** (n=516 vs 823) — mild reversal. **W≥20000:** essentially all events have some extract within window; pooled mean **~+0.30**. First extract after basket within 100000: dominated by **Mark55↔Mark14** and **Mark55↔Mark01** pairs (see JSON top list). **trader_v11** (extract spread **2 or 3** only + joint gate): total **636** vs v2 **13,236** — mid-width extract slice is **too sparse** in the sim for this passive style; prefer v2 or v10 band.

round4_post_phase3_notes.leadlag_windows: W in raw tape timestamp units; next extract = first extract trade with ts_extract > ts_vev same csv day.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v11_vs_v2: Spread {2,3} filter collapses activity and PnL.

analysis_description: Phase 1 extensions (post-gate workstream): (1) adjacent-trade two-hop graph motif A→B→C when seller_i==buyer_{i+1}, extract fwd20 at second leg vs control; (2) day-block bootstrap CI for top participant (party,symbol,role) cells K=20 on traded symbol. Sim: trader_v12 = duplicate of v2 for reproducible iteration log.
used_to_decide: **Two-hop (r4_twohop_adjacent_extract_fwd20.json):** Pooled extract fwd20 at second leg is **negative** for middle-name-matched adjacent pairs (**mean −0.24**, n=686) vs **+0.26** for other adjacent pairs (n=3590) — **falsifies** using naive CSV-adjacency as a positive scheduler signal for extract; dominant motifs are **Mark14↔Mark38** hydro-related chains. **Bootstrap:** Mark **67** buyer_agg **EXTRACT** pooled mean **~1.81**, cluster-bootstrap 95% CI **[1.45, 2.03]** — **strictly positive** under day resampling; Mark **55** buyer_agg extract CI **[0.33, 0.57]** excludes 0; Mark **14** buyer_agg hydro CI still straddles 0. **trader_v12** (v2 duplicate): **13,236** worse=all, **4365** trades — confirms baseline.

round4_post_phase3_notes.phase1_gate_update: round4_phase1_complete iteration-0 bullets 1 and 3 augmented with new output paths and conclusions.
round4_post_phase3_notes.trader_v12: Identical logic to trader_v2; separate traderData key r4_iter12_gate_only.

analysis_description: Phase 1 bullet 5 extension: passive-side (non-aggressor) markout by (Mark, symbol) K=20; graph reciprocity for top directed edges. Sim: trader_v13 relaxes Sonic joint gate to L1<=3 on 5200 and 5300 (ablation vs v2 TH=2).
used_to_decide: **Passive markouts (r4_passive_side_markout_by_party_k20.json):** Mark14/Mark01 as **passive** on **VEV_5300** show the most **negative** mean forwards on the **traded** mid (worst cell Mark14/5300 **−0.25**, n=28); Mark22/Mark49 passive on **extract** remain strongly **positive** — use as **avoid** vs **OK to get hit** heuristic, not full book L2. **Reciprocity:** **M01→M22** has **no** reverse prints; **14↔38** and **14↔55** ~**0.95+** reciprocity (duopoly). **trader_v13** (both core strikes L1≤**3**): total **6,694** vs v2 **13,236** — **loosening** Sonic’s ≤2 **hurts** sim PnL on this tape; keep **2** for execution.

round4_post_phase3_notes.round4_phase1_complete_updated: Bullets 3 and 5 in iteration-0 object now list reciprocity + passive markout files.
round4_post_phase3_notes.sonic_threshold_sensitivity: v13 worse=all 6694; supports tight ≤2 as in STRATEGY not arbitrary.
